title: Hi.Events
description: Open-source event management and ticket-selling platform (Laravel + React) for creating events, selling tickets, and managing attendees.
installation: Hi.Events is a Laravel API + React frontend stack. This template deploys the ALL-IN-ONE image (daveearley/hi.events-all-in-one, container port 80) which bundles the API, the built frontend, nginx, and the queue worker + scheduler in one container, so for a basic deployment NO companion services are required. It is wired to the Postgres and Valkey (Redis) add-ons provisioned here. For a horizontally-scaled production setup you can instead split into separate images, all sharing the SAME DATABASE_URL, Redis and APP_KEY/JWT_SECRET: (1) Backend API - image daveearley/hi.events-backend on port 8000; (2) a queue/scheduler worker from the same backend image with command 'php artisan queue:work' plus a scheduler ('php artisan schedule:work'); (3) Frontend - image daveearley/hi.events-frontend on port 3000, set its VITE_API_URL_CLIENT/VITE_API_URL_SERVER to the backend URL. ROTATE before exposing publicly: APP_KEY (a 32-char value prefixed with 'base64:', e.g. `echo "base64:$(openssl rand -base64 32)"`), JWT_SECRET (`openssl rand -hex 32`), and the Postgres add-on password. SMTP is required to deliver tickets and confirmation emails: set MAIL_HOST/MAIL_PORT/MAIL_USERNAME/MAIL_PASSWORD to a real relay. Payments use Stripe: set STRIPE_SECRET_KEY / STRIPE_PUBLIC_KEY / STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET and VITE_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY. The public URLs (APP_FRONTEND_URL / VITE_FRONTEND_URL / APP_URL) are wired to this app's public URL. First run: the container runs migrations on boot; then browse to the site and register the first organiser account (set APP_DISABLE_REGISTRATION=true afterwards to close signups). Ticket images/logos and other uploads are persisted on the mounted volume.
